title: Cymatic Planner PASS: BFS finds shortest path flat -> stripes -> hexagons in k=2
_fixture_purpose: Demonstrates successful BFS planning: starting from flat (QA singularity), the planner finds the shortest legal 2-step path to hexagons (QA cosmos) via the required stripes intermediate. optimization_goal=shortest_path is verified: BFS guarantees no 1-step path exists (flat has no direct edge to hexagons), so k=2 is minimal. QA analogue: shortest generator-sequence walk from singularity orbit to cosmos orbit, constrained to pass through satellite threshold.

qa_mapping.planning_qa_analogue: BFS over the cymatic control graph is exactly bounded reachability search in the QA orbit graph. Each generator move (increase_amplitude, set_frequency) corresponds to one application of the QA generator T. Flat=singularity is the fixed-point orbit (period 1); stripes=satellite is the 8-cycle orbit (threshold crossing); hexagons=cosmos is the 24-cycle orbit. The planner certifies: min-step path from singularity to cosmos in the finite orbit graph has length 2, passing through the satellite threshold — matching the QA invariant that no single T-step can bridge singularity to cosmos without satellite crossing.

validation_checks.details: bfs in {bfs, dfs, astar, dijkstra, iddfs}

validation_checks.description: every plan step is a legal edge in control_graph
observed final pattern class matches target pattern class
shortest-path claim consistent: BFS recomputed shortest == claimed path_length_k
validation_checks.details: BFS recomputes shortest path flat->hexagons within bound 3 = 2; claimed path_length_k=2; 2 == 2

validation_checks.description: no disordered/out_of_orbit state visited in plan
validation_checks.details: plan visits only flat, stripes, hexagons — none disordered
